avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to gradually consolidate in the fluctuating temperatures, snowpack stability will be good. Many slopes will be firm and icy particualry slopes above 900 metres exposed to the recent South-Easterly winds, elsewhere a breakable crust is likely to from as the freezing level falls. Any new snow amounts are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Mainly dry with occasional light showers which will fall as snow above around 500 metres. The freezing level will fall from around 800 metres to 400 metres during the forecast period. Gale force mainly Easterly winds on the summits. .
Observed Weather Influences: Damp with occasional drizzle or summit snow particularly where in the cloud. The freezing level was around 950 metres although temperatures only just below freezing on the highest summits. Moderate South-Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Slopes scoured by the recent South-Easterly winds are scoured, often icy and stable. Older windslab which exists mainly  on West to North aspects above 950 metres is now well bonded and stability here is also generally good. Cornices above these aspects are fragile. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of consolidated windslab will be present, mainly on steep West, North-West and North aspects above 1000m. Elsewhere the snowpack will be generally refrozen and stable.  The avalanche hazard will be Low

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will be dry with fresh to strong Easterly winds over the summits. The freezing level will gradually lower to around 400m during Tuesday
Observed Weather Influences: It has been a mostly dry day with some light precipitation during the afternoon. Freezing level was around 900m.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of well consolidated windslab are present, mainly on steep West, North-West and North aspects above 1000m. 
The snow surface is generally frozen on slopes exposed to the South-Easterly winds. There is a gradual thaw below 900m.  The avalanche hazard is Low

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh deposits of unstable windslab will be present in wind sheltered locations on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres, The greatest instability will be in steep locations such as coire headwalls, gullies and convex shaped terrain. Unstable cornices will overhang these aspects. Between 500 and 900 metres, more localised instabilities will be present on steep banks and burn lines. Wind exposed slopes will be firm. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Very strong East-South-East winds during the evening will back Easterly overnight and persist for the remainder of the day. Precipitation throughout the forecast period will be heaviest overnight and into the morning, falling as snow initially to 500 metres, then 100 metres by the end of the day as the freezing level gradually lowers.
Observed Weather Influences: Moderate snowfall above 650 metres for most of the day. The freezing level was around 750 metres. Very strong East-South-East winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Recent fluctuating temperatures have encouraged consolidation with much snow demonstrating good stability. In wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects above 900 metres, fresh unstable windslab continues to develop. Deeper accumulations of older firm windslab contain moderately bonded layers. Large cornices are present above these aspects. Wind exposed slopes are firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will be hard, icy and stable throughout. Greatest snow amounts are predominantly on North-West through North and East to Southerly aspects above 700m. Any fresh windslab deposits will be isolated and shallow. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Very strong to gale force Easterly winds. The freezing level will be around 700 metres initially, dropping overnight to 400 metres where it will remain. Light precipitation expected over the inland ranges, drier over the coastal mountains.
Observed Weather Influences: Dry with the freezing level around 850 metres. Light Easterly winds initially, strengthening during the day to become strong.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is firm and stable throughout. Greatest snow amounts are predominantly on North-West through North and East to Southerly aspects above 700m. Elsewhere, snow is confined to gullies and patches. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to consolidate and stability will be generally good. Very isolated and shallow areas of windslab will be present in sheltered locations mainly on South-West through West to North aspects above 950 metres. Some fragile cornices will be present, mainly above these aspects. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: There will be very strong Easterly winds. Mainly dry with isolated showers falling as snow above 300 metres. The freezing level will drop to around 400 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Occasional very light showers falling as snow above 800 metres. Moderate South-Easterly winds. The freezing level was around 950 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to consolidate and stability is generally good. Very isolated and shallow areas of windslab are present in sheltered locations mainly on West to North aspects above 950 metres. Some fragile cornices are present, mainly above these aspects. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New accumulations of windslab will develop on West to North aspects above 900 metres. The greatest instability will be located in areas such as corrie head walls, gullies and convexities.  Cross loading will affect South-West and North-East aspects. Fragile cornices will continue to develop on these same aspects. Below 900 meters windslab is also expected to build in areas such as steep banks, convexities and burn lines. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force East-South-East winds. Snow showers throughout the forecast period. The freezing level will initially be around 600 meters, lowering to 400 meters by late afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Moderate South-Easterly winds increased to very strong by mid-day. Heavy snow showers from mid-morning onwards. The freezing level lowered to around 800 meters by late afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh deposits of unstable windslab developed on West to North aspects above 900 meters from mid-morning onwards. South-West and North-East aspects are also affected due to cross loading. Areas most affected are wind-sheltered locations such as corrie rims and the tops of gullies. Fragile cornices are present above these same aspects. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
